data.title: Crypto service provider SOS Limited has temporarily shut down its internal Bitcoin mining operations.
data.description: SOS Limited, a New York Stock Exchange-listed crypto services provider engaged in commodities trading, cryptocurrency mining, and hosting, disclosed in its latest 2025 semi-annual financial results that it has temporarily closed its internal Bitcoin mining operations (self-mining). The company, which raised $7.5 million through a rights offering at the end of July, plans to prioritize the development and expansion of its third-party mining hosting services. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Aave founder Stani is suspected of selling 4 million ENA obtained from investment
data.description: On-chain analyst Ember Monitoring suggests that Aave founder Stani.eth may have sold 4 million ENA (US$2.38 million) from his investment: Stani.eth transferred 4 million ENA to the institutional trading platform Galaxy Digital six hours ago. Stani is reportedly an investor in Ethena, and these ENA were received from unlocking the Ethena wallet.

data.title: 0G Foundation: Airdrops have been distributed to testnet node operators
data.description: The 0G Foundation officially announced on X that it has conducted an airdrop for testnet node operators, and the airdrop was sent directly to the wallets of the airdrop recipients on the 0G mainnet.

data.title: The ETF Store President: Multiple institutions have submitted S-1 amendments for spot SOL ETFs, which may be approved within two weeks
data.description: Nate Geraci, president of The ETF Store, posted on X that a series of S-1 amendments for spot SOL ETFs were submitted today, including from Franklin, Fidelity, CoinShares, Bitwise, Grayscale, VanEck, and Canary. These amendments include collateralization (which bodes well for the introduction of collateralization in spot ETH ETFs). These amendments are expected to be approved within the next two weeks.

data.title: Plasma Blockchain Surges in DeFi Rankings with $4 Billion in 24 Hours
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the stablecoin Layer 1 blockchain Plasma has absorbed over $4 billion in cryptocurrency within 24 hours of its launch, securing the eighth position in blockchain DeFi deposit value rankings. This achievement is largely attributed to the Plasma lending vault and its associated DeFi protocols, where users can earn the network's native token, XPL.

The initial success of Plasma has driven the price of the XPL token up by 30% from its opening price, currently valued at approximately $1.2. This corresponds to a fully diluted valuation of nearly $12 billion, marking a 24-fold increase from its $50 million valuation during its public sale in June. For investors who participated in Plasma's seed round on the early investment platform Echo last November, this represents an astonishing 324-fold return.

data.title: WLFI Executes Significant Token Purchase and Burn Strategy
data.description:
According to Foresight News, WLFI recently conducted a substantial on-chain transaction involving the purchase and subsequent destruction of its tokens. Five hours ago, WLFI acquired 3.814 million WLFI tokens for $798,000 at a price of $0.21 per token. Following this purchase, WLFI proceeded to burn a total of 6.923 million WLFI tokens, valued at $1.47 million.

The company stated that the burned tokens included the newly acquired 3.814 million WLFI tokens, along with an additional 3.109 million WLFI tokens, which were part of the protocol's fee revenue. This strategic move reflects WLFI's ongoing efforts to manage its token supply and enhance its economic model.

data.title: Kraken may introduce new strategic investors with a valuation of $20 billion and plans to raise $200-300 million
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken is in talks for a pre-IPO funding round that could value the company at $20 billion. Bloomberg, citing anonymous sources, reports that the fundraising, which has not yet been finalized and remains subject to market conditions, would involve a strategic investor contributing between $200 million and $300 million. (Bloomberg)

data.title: WLFI repurchased 3.814 million tokens and destroyed 6.923 million WLFI
data.description: On-chain analyst Ember Monitoring reported that WLFI purchased 3.814 million WLFI on-chain for $0.21 for $798,000 five hours ago. They then destroyed 6.923 million WLFI (worth $1.47 million). WLFI claimed the 6.923 million WLFI destroyed included the 3.814 million WLFI it had just purchased and 3.109 million WLFI from protocol fees.

data.title: The Mobility Advantage: Why Bitcoin’s Portability Makes It Superior To Traditional Gold
data.description:
Bitcoin and Gold as stores of value often boil down to a single, critical distinction in the digital era of mobility. This portability transforms BTC from just a digital gold narrative into a living, breathing monetary network that gold can never match. According to mhar_leeck’s perspective on X, the true evolution of BTC lies in its capacity as a platform for innovation, to move, evolve, and even teach. Unlike gold, which stays locked away, this narrative confines the asset to a passive role
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-mobility-advantage/
